events.text: Linda Rodriguez, 62-year-old Other female, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with High-grade serous carcinoma of the Right ovary. Clinical staging: T3cN0M0, AJCC Stage IIIC. ICD-10: C56.9. Stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian cancer with BRCA1 mutation.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Underwent primary debulking surgery: Total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, omentectomy, and optimal cytoreduction (residual disease < 1 cm). Estimated blood loss 150 mL. Length of stay 3 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 anemia. Hemoglobin 8.5 g/dL. Transfused 2 units pRBC. Iron studies checked.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic: Carboplatin AUC 6 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 q3w x 6 cycles + Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
PET/CT performed for restaging after first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line chemotherapy with anti-angiogenic.
Initiated PARP inhibitor maintenance: Olaparib 300 mg BID maintenance. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on PARP inhibitor maintenance.
Clinical visit for follow-up on PARP inhibitor maintenance.
PET/CT performed for restaging on PARP inhibitor maintenance.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Linda Rodriguez
MRN: patient_19578737892d
Date of Study: 2023-05-31

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Complex cystic and solid mass in the right adnexa measuring 8.0 cm. Omental caking present. Moderate ascites. Peritoneal carcinomatosis.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Complex adnexal mass with peritoneal carcinomatosis and ascites, concerning for advanced ovarian malignancy.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Linda Rodriguez
MRN: patient_19578737892d
Specimen ID: S2023-51746
Date of Procedure: 2023-06-04
Date of Report: 2023-06-06

SPECIMEN: Right ovary, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade serous carcinoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 3.8 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
WT1: Positive
P53: Aberrant (overexpression)
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Linda Rodriguez - Right ovary" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 3.8 x 2.7 x 1.9 cm. The cut surface reveals a indurated gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A6.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Gynecologic Oncology

Patient: Linda Rodriguez
MRN: patient_19578737892d
Date: 2023-06-08
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Rodriguez is a 62-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary, IIIC. Diagnosis made following evaluation of progressive symptoms. Stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian cancer with BRCA1 mutation. Pathology confirmed high-grade serous carcinoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports palpable mass noted by patient.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- hypothyroidism
- hyperlipidemia

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Appendectomy (age 25)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Current smoker (1 ppd)
- Alcohol: Social drinker (2-3 drinks/week)
- Occupation: Retired teacher
- Lives with spouse and adult children

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with colon cancer at age 70.

ALLERGIES: Iodine contrast (anaphylaxis)

MEDICATIONS:
- Levothyroxine 75 mcg daily
- Atorvastatin 40 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild dyspnea on exertion, intermittent headaches. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 142/74, HR 85, RR 16, Temp 97.7F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 75.4 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 6.2, Hgb 14.4, Plt 133, Cr 0.84

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
62-year-old female with IIIC high-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. initial oncology consultation.

1. High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary, Stage IIIC
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend primary debulking surgery
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Port placement scheduled
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Richard Okafor, MD
Gynecologic Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Linda Rodriguez
MRN: patient_19578737892d
Date of Report: 2023-06-19
Specimen: Right ovary (Resection)
Diagnosis: High-grade serous carcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 4.0 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  BRCA1 p.E1836fs (c.5503delA) - Frameshift (Exon 22) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  NF1 p.R1947* - Nonsense (Exon 39)
  TP53 p.C176Y - Missense (Exon 5)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CDK12 p.T826A - VUS
  ARID1A p.D1850N - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - BRCA1 pathogenic variant: FDA-approved PARP inhibitors include olaparib, niraparib, rucaparib.
  - Platinum sensitivity expected.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 427x coverage.
Tumor content: 46%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Linda Rodriguez
MRN: patient_19578737892d
Specimen ID: S2023-47191
Date of Procedure: 2023-07-03
Date of Report: 2023-07-07

SPECIMEN: Right ovary, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade serous carcinoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
WT1: Positive
P53: Aberrant (overexpression)
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Linda Rodriguez - Right ovary" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 5.2 x 3.6 x 2.6 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Sarah Chen, MD
Department of Pathology

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Linda Rodriguez
MRN: patient_19578737892d
Date of Study: 2024-11-30

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade serous carcinoma of the right ovary. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Prior study dated approximately 11 weeks ago available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Complex cystic and solid mass in the right adnexa measuring 8.8 cm. Omental caking present. Moderate ascites. Peritoneal carcinomatosis.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Complex adnexal mass with peritoneal carcinomatosis and ascites, concerning for advanced ovarian malignancy.
2. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology
